name: SF2B Author continuity lock rejects replacement chapter frame

source.purpose: Ensures SF2B Ch2 Author validation rejects a locally plausible replacement broker/location/pressure that fails to preserve Calla Voss, Sable Notch, Redline/Carrow, and the unresolved tail/cutter pressure.

thread_tail_cutter.title: The unresolved tail and cutter
thread_tail_cutter.resolutionCriteria: Identify whether the cutter shadowing Redline/Carrow belongs to Calla, a rival, or the cargo's owner.
thread_tail_cutter.failureMode: The cutter forces the route before the cargo terms are understood.
thread_tail_cutter.retrievalCue: tail, cutter silhouette, sealed Redline/Carrow cargo

thread_redline_carrow_cargo.title: The sealed Redline/Carrow cargo
thread_redline_carrow_cargo.resolutionCriteria: Determine what obligation the sealed cargo creates before leaving Sable Notch.
thread_redline_carrow_cargo.failureMode: The cargo becomes leverage before the PC knows who owns the risk.
thread_redline_carrow_cargo.retrievalCue: sealed cargo, Redline/Carrow route, Calla's brokerage

npc_calla_voss.name: Calla Voss

voice.note: smiles only after the price lands

npc_calla_voss.retrievalCue: Calla Voss, broker, sealed Redline/Carrow cargo

loc_sable_notch.name: Sable Notch
loc_sable_notch.description: A hard-edged transfer port where cargo jobs become debts.

promise_calla_delivery.obligation: Do not open or reroute the sealed cargo before Calla names the buyer.

sceneSummaries.summary: Calla Voss left the PC at Sable Notch with sealed Redline/Carrow cargo and an unresolved cutter tail.

currentLocation.name: Sable Notch
currentLocation.description: A hard-edged transfer port where cargo jobs become debts.

location.name: Sable Notch
location.description: A hard-edged transfer port where cargo jobs become debts.

established: Calla Voss brokered the sealed Redline/Carrow cargo.
The PC is still at Sable Notch.
A cutter tail remains unresolved outside the port lane.

turns.playerInput: I take the job but keep watching the cutter.
turns.narratorProse: Calla Voss lets the sealed Redline/Carrow crate rest between you while Sable Notch's lane glass catches the cutter's shadow.

chapter_frame.title: Dawn Ledger
chapter_frame.premise: A new port broker forces the PC to move a nameless container before a patrol sweep.
chapter_frame.active_pressure: A patrol-backed broker turns transit into a loyalty test.
chapter_frame.central_tension: Move fast or learn who is using the PC.
chapter_frame.chapter_scope: One port queue and one replacement broker.
chapter_frame.objective: Decide whether to accept Kira's new container job.
chapter_frame.crucible: The PC must choose speed or scrutiny under patrol pressure.

outcome_spectrum.clean: The container clears without exposing the PC.
outcome_spectrum.costly: The container clears but Kira claims leverage.
outcome_spectrum.failure: The patrol marks the PC as noncompliant.
outcome_spectrum.catastrophic: The container opens into a faction seizure.

opening_scene_spec.initial_state: Kira Maal has a new container already tagged to the PC's hull.
opening_scene_spec.first_player_facing: Kira asks whether the PC wants speed or explanations.
opening_scene_spec.immediate_choice: Accept Kira's terms or demand the manifest.
withheld_premise_facts: The patrol knows more than Kira admits.

antagonist_field.core_pressure: A sealed container cannot wait in the queue.

possible_faces.name: Kira Maal
possible_faces.becomes_primary_when: The PC challenges her container terms.

antagonist_field.escalation_logic: Every delay gives the patrol more authority over the hull.

starting_npcs.name: Kira Maal
starting_npcs.voice_note: laughs before every hard term lands
starting_npcs.hidden_pressure: Needs the PC to carry patrol evidence.
starting_npcs.disposition_reason: she sees the operative as useful but uncontrolled

starting_npcs.name: Patrol Lio
starting_npcs.hidden_pressure: Already has a seizure order drafted.
starting_npcs.disposition_reason: the patrol treats unverified haulers as seizure targets

starting_npcs.name: Jex Orro
starting_npcs.disposition_reason: he has no prior tie and wants to avoid patrol attention

active_threads.title: Kira's container
active_threads.question: Will the PC move Kira's container?
active_threads.resolution_criteria: The PC accepts, rejects, or exposes Kira's container terms.
active_threads.failure_mode: The patrol locks the hull under compliance authority.

active_threads.title: The patrol sweep
active_threads.question: Can the PC clear the queue?
active_threads.resolution_criteria: The queue clears or the PC is detained.
active_threads.failure_mode: Detention turns transit into a seizure.

active_threads.title: The manifest gap
active_threads.question: Who switched the manifest?
active_threads.resolution_criteria: The switch is named or made irrelevant.
active_threads.failure_mode: The switch becomes evidence against the PC.

active_threads.title: Free hauler debt
active_threads.question: Will Jex owe the PC?
active_threads.resolution_criteria: Jex helps or refuses in public.
active_threads.failure_mode: Jex disappears before the tag switch matters.

arc_link.chapter_function: Complicate cargo transit with public pressure.
arc_link.player_stance_read: The PC favors questions before speed.

pacing_contract.chapter_question: Will the PC accept the new container pressure?

continuation_moves.prior_chapter_meaning: The PC became useful to cargo brokers.
continuation_moves.larger_pattern_revealed: Ports use sealed freight as loyalty tests.

worsened_existing_thread.why_load_bearing_now: the seal lets Kira control what can be known

planted_midchapter_revelation.hidden_statement: The patrol planted the container tag.
planted_midchapter_revelation.recontextualizes: Kira is a pressure face, not the full source.

pressure_ladder.pressure: The queue counter assigns the hull.
pressure_ladder.trigger_condition: Kira formally invokes the patrol tag.
pressure_ladder.narrative_effect: Choice becomes visible to the whole queue.
The PC must choose evidence or motion.
The hull becomes the bargaining chip.

tension_score.pressure: Kira needs the PC to move the container now.
tension_score.prose_surface: She treats hesitation as already expensive.
tension_score.advances_when: The PC delays or demands terms.
tension_score.resolves_or_reframes_when: The PC accepts, rejects, or exposes her terms.

tension_score.prose_surface: Her friendliness sharpens around every refusal.
tension_score.advances_when: The PC asks for trust without compliance.
tension_score.resolves_or_reframes_when: Kira is exposed or becomes useful.

tension_score.pressure: The patrol can seize the hull.
tension_score.prose_surface: Lio's stamps arrive before his words.

possible_revelations.statement: The patrol planted the container tag.
possible_revelations.recontextualizes: Kira is not the full author of pressure.

possible_revelations.statement: Kira owes the patrol a seizure.
possible_revelations.emergence_condition: Kira loses control of the queue.
possible_revelations.recontextualizes: Her urgency is debt, not only profit.

moral_fault_lines.why_it_hurts: Each ally can become the owner.

escalation_options.consequence: The queue starts judging the hull.
The tag becomes harder to alter.

editorialized_lore.relevance_now: The tag gives patrol pressure teeth.
editorialized_lore.delivery_method: Lio points to the tag, not Kira.
Jex watches who hears each answer.
